definition: I2S substitutes a specific named constant (4-byte FOURCC, chunk type, table tag, colorspace signature, or protocol next-header byte) into a single dispatch field, routing execution into a particular handler or switch arm within an already-recognized format.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S intercepts the switch CMP on e_machine, logs the EM_X86_64 constant 0x3e, and substitutes it at input offset 0x12-0x13; naive must blindly guess the 2-byte value and almost never does.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes EM_X86_64 constant into e_machine field to hit the switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF header e_machine field (2 bytes at offset 0x12) must equal EM_X86_64 (0x003e) to take the case EM_X86_64 switch arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 2-byte little-endian e_machine field at input offset 0x12-0x13 must hold 0x003e (62 = EM_X86_64). The switch on e_machine at elf.cc:782 dispatches the true arm only for this exact 16-bit value.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seed 2a25258f656af261 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x12-0x13 (little-endian 0x003e = EM_X86_64), and seed 80ff9a7dac09a5e8 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x13-0x14; both supply the exact comparand the switch tests for.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds carry 00 at offset 0x12, never the 0x3e byte that the switch arm requires, so they fall through to the default arm and return false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the switch comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp callbacks, logs the constant 0x3e operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact byte into the input at the e_machine offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must hit 0x003e by blind havoc mutation, which it almost never does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the case EM_X86_64 arm of a switch on e_machine.

W bytes at offset 0x12 include 0x3e while L bytes there are dominated by 00.
Loser naive seed holds 00 at offset 0x12 and never the EM_X86_64 byte.
The loser takes the default arm and returns false at line 822/828.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S logs the EM_AARCH64 constant 0xB7 from the switch CMP callbacks and substitutes it at input offset 0x12, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step that naive never hits by blind mutation.
signature.one_line: I2S writes EM_AARCH64 byte into e_machine offset to dispatch the correct switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF e_machine field must equal EM_AARCH64 (0x00B7 little-endian) at the 16-bit machine offset to take the switch arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ELF header e_machine half-word must hold the exact 16-bit value EM_AARCH64 = 0x00B7 (low byte 0xB7, high byte 0x00) so that `switch (e_machine)` dispatches to `case EM_AARCH64:` at elf.cc:798. In the winner this lands at input bytes 0x12=0xB7, 0x13=0x00.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed carries the bytes 01 d5 b7 00 at offsets 0x10-0x13, placing 0xB7 at offset 0x12; the per-role overlay marks line 798 case EM_AARCH64 as [W] (winner-only) and the divergent-branch table shows W takes the true side (T=3 F=0) while L never does (T=0 F=29).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed misses the EM_AARCH64 constant: the byte-diff shows offset 0x12 is 0xB7 only in W while L holds 00x8/03/01, so the switch falls through to `default:` (line 822, marked [L]) which returns false; the divergent-branch table shows L takes default true 26 times and EM_AARCH64 never.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CMP-interception logs the `e_machine == EM_AARCH64` integer comparison operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the observed operand and substitutes the magic 0xB7 directly at the matching offset, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step. naive has only havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it must blind-guess the 2-byte magic and never lands it across 10 trials.

Winner takes the EM_AARCH64 true side while the loser never does.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer recording the scheme strcmp against 'file', then substitutes the 'file://' literal into the input at offset 0x06, routing curl_url_set to return CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL and selecting the case arm at strerror.c:529.
signature.one_line: I2S inserts 'file://' scheme to trigger CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL case arm

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a literal 'file://' (case-insensitive) URL scheme so curl_url_set rejects with CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL, hitting the case arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must carry a literal 'file://' (or 'FILE://') scheme prefix at the URL field (seen at offset 0x06 onward) that drives curl_url_set down the file-URL handling path and produces a CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL error code, which is the only value selecting the case CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL arm at strerror.c:529.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed contains the exact ASCII scheme token 'file://'/'FILE://' starting at offset 0x06 (e.g. seed 1 '66 69 6c 65 3a 2f 2f' = file://, seed 2 '46 49 4c 45 3a 2f 2f' = FILE://). cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and the winner takes the true side (W 529 T=10 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no scheme token at offsets 0x06-0x0c — they hold byte runs like '70 6b 2b 2b' (pk++), '24 25 41 25' ($%A%) and percent/punctuation noise, so curl_url_set never returns CURLUE_BAD_FILE_URL and the case arm at 529 is never taken (L 529 T=0 F=10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recorded when curl_url_set's scheme comparison (a strcmp/memcmp against the 'file' literal) executes, scans the input for the matched operand, and substitutes the 'file' constant at those offsets. naive lacks this CMP introspection and must land the multi-byte scheme literal by blind havoc, which it manages in only 2/10 trials.

A second winner seed carries the uppercase 'FILE://' scheme token in the same position.
Across W seeds offsets 0x06-0x09 concentrate on the f/i/l/e (and F/I/L/E) bytes while L seeds spread over percent/punctuation noise.
Loser-blocking seeds lack any scheme token, carrying punctuation/percent noise at the scheme offsets instead.
Winner cmplog takes the true side 10/10 while loser naive takes it 0/10.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the COLR version!=1 comparison and 'COLR' tag comparison were recorded, then substitutes the constant 1 at the version field and 'COLR' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, making version==1 false and routing execution into the COLRv1 extents path.
signature.one_line: I2S places 'COLR' tag and version==1 to enter COLRv1 extents path

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed COLR table header with COLRv1 version field equal to 1 at the parsed version offset, so version != 1 takes its false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A sfnt header whose table-directory entry tags the four ASCII bytes 'COLR' (offsets 0x0c-0x0f) AND whose COLR table parses to version == 1, so the equality test version != 1 evaluates false and execution falls through into the COLRv1 extents/paint path (lines 1993-2024).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII 'COLR' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and a stable 00 01 00 00 00 01 header prefix at 0x00-0x05, and the winner hits the false branch 30 times (W T=0 F=30) while resolving the COLRv1 path (get_clip / paint_glyph functions show 51-176 W hits vs 0 L hits).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry varied non-magic bytes at 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. 00/01/2c/2b instead of 43 4f 4c 52) and never satisfy version == 1, so they take the true branch (L T=21 F=0) and return false at line 1991 before any COLRv1 code runs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator (absent in value_profile): the CmpLogObserver records the version != 1 comparison operands, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant 1 directly into the input at the version offset, deterministically driving version to 1 — value_profile only gets a CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient bucket, which nudges but does not place the exact value, so it stays blocked all 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the equality test version != 1 and the globally blocked side is the false branch.

Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII COLR tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
Across all winner seeds the COLR magic occupies offsets 0x0c-0x0f while loser seeds carry varied non-magic bytes there.
On the blocker line the winner takes the false branch 30 times while the loser takes the true branch 21 times.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte offset of the parsed version field is inferred from the COLR tag position and header prefix, not directly read from source, so the precise dataflow offset I2S must hit is uncertain.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from the 'sbix' table-tag comparison and substitutes the 4-byte literal 'sbix' (73 62 69 78) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, giving has_data() a real strike table and taking the false branch so PNG-extent code runs.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 4-byte 'sbix' tag so has_data() returns true

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a well-formed sbix table (correct 'sbix' tag at the table directory offset) so has_data() returns true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid OpenType font whose table directory names a real 'sbix' table (ASCII tag 73 62 69 78 = 'sbix') at the offset has_data() inspects, so the accelerator binds sbix data and !has_data() is false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed contains the literal ASCII 'sbix' tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (73 62 69 78) plus a sfnt header (00 01 00 00 = version 1.0), giving has_data() a non-empty strike table so the F (false) branch is taken and downstream PNG-extent code runs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short/garbage (e.g. 6 bytes, or all-zero/random) with no 'sbix' tag at 0x0c-0x0f, so has_data() returns false, the T (true) branch returns false, and the function exits before reaching get_png_extents body.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the sbix table-tag comparison logged the expected operand 'sbix', then substitutes that 4-byte literal into the input at the matching offset; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot plant the exact 4-byte magic, so they stay blocked.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 4-byte table-tag comparison against 'MATH', records the constant in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes it at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, routing the harness into the MATH parse path so Coverage::get_coverage reaches a non-default switch arm.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'MATH' FOURCC to route into MATH table Coverage path

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the literal 'MATH' table-tag FOURCC at offset 0x0c so the OT MATH parser reaches Coverage::get_coverage with a valid format.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes at offset 0x0c-0x0f must equal the ASCII FOURCC 'MATH' (0x4d 0x41 0x54 0x48), the table tag that routes the harness into the OT MATH table sanitize/parse path which constructs Coverage objects and calls Coverage::get_coverage; without this exact 4-byte tag the MATH table is never selected and the false branch (a successfully-parsed coverage format 1-4) is never reached.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry 'MATH' verbatim at offset 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. seed 04299261480ace96 shows '4d 41 54 48  MATH'), and the byte-diff shows offsets 0x0c-0x0f concentrated on M/A/T/H in W versus scattered bytes in L; cmplog/vpc's I2S substitution copies the literal table-tag operand seen in the tag comparison into the input.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds lack the 'MATH' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. naive seed 003817559785f774 is only 6 bytes; value_profile seeds carry unrelated bytes there), so they never enter the MATH parse path and Coverage::get_coverage is hit only via the default switch taking the true (NOT_COVERED) side, never the false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the 4-byte table-tag comparison, record the constant 'MATH' operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that literal at the matching input offset; naive and value_profile have no I2S mutator and must stumble onto the exact 32-bit FOURCC by chance, which they fail to do across all 10 trials.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the GDEF tag memcmp and version-major comparison, substitutes 'GDEF' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and 0x0001 at the version field, routing the switch to case 1/case 2 and avoiding the default false return.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a GDEF table whose version.major equals 1 or 2 so has_glyph_classes() avoids the default false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A GDEF table header whose 16-bit major-version field decodes to 1 or 2 (bytes 00 01 at the version offset) together with the 'GDEF' tag, so the switch in has_glyph_classes() dispatches to case 1 or case 2 instead of the default arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins 00 01 00 00 ... 47 44 45 46 ('GDEF'), giving version.major=1, so the source switch reaches case 1 (line 680, T=399) or case 2 (line 682) and the blocker default branch is taken False (W: line 684 F=468, never True).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no valid GDEF version header (e.g. 87 0e 0d 0e, fe ff ff ff, e0 17 00 00) so version.major is neither 1 nor 2; the switch falls through to default: return false, taking the blocker True side (L: line 684 T=440).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands from the version-major comparison and the 'GDEF' tag memcmp, then substitutes the exact constant (01 for major, the GDEF FOURCC) into the input at the matching offsets; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot guess these exact multi-byte magic constants by havoc or Hamming-distance feedback alone.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the GDEF table-tag and version comparisons, substitutes the 'GDEF' FOURCC at 0x0c-0x0f and 00 01 version at 0x00-0x01, so the GDEF sanitize chain runs and get_glyph_class_def hits case 1 rather than the Null(ClassDef) default.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'GDEF' tag and version 1 so get_glyph_class_def avoids default

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a valid GDEF table header with version.major == 1 or 2 so the switch dispatches off the default Null case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt/GDEF font header: the leading bytes must encode a recognized table version (u.version.major == 1, i.e. bytes 00 01 at the GDEF table start) and the input must contain the 'GDEF' (47 44 45 46) table tag so the GDEF table sanitizes and get_glyph_class_def reaches case 1 / case 2 of switch (u.version.major) instead of the default Null(ClassDef) blocker.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with 00 01 00 00 00 01 and contains the literal 'GDEF' tag (47 44 45 46) at offset 0x0c-0x0f; the W byte set at those offsets is fully concentrated (47(G)x20, 44(D)x20, 45(E)x20, 46(F)x20), and at runtime line 690 case 1 is taken (T side reached) so the false default branch at line 694 is exercised.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds lack the GDEF tag at 0x0c-0x0f (L bytes there are scattered: 00, 20, junk) and lack the 00 01 version header, so the GDEF table never sanitizes (all GDEF sanitize functions show L hits = 0) and get_glyph_class_def is never reached; the blocker false branch stays at F=0 for losers.

hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the exact sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 (offsets 0x00-0x03 hold 00 01 00 00 in all 20 W samples) and carries an ASCII table tag GPOS or GSUB at offset 0x0c (W concentrates 47 'G' x20, 50 'P'/53 'S', 4f 'O', 53 'S'/42 'B'). These magic/tag bytes are CMP-gated equality comparisons against the literal table tags, satisfied by I2S substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short and structurally invalid: offset 0x0c carries scattered bytes (00/01/20/2c, +11 unique) with no GPOS/GSUB FOURCC, and the sfnt magic offsets 0x00-0x03 hold heterogeneous bytes (e0/05/87/0e...). Without the magic+tag the parser never reaches the version switch, so the default branch stays unhit and L blocks 10/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands from the sfnt-magic and FOURCC tag-table equality comparisons and substitutes the exact required literals (00 01 00 00, 'GPOS'/'GSUB') at the matching offsets in one mutation, jumping a multi-byte equality wall that naive/value_profile havoc cannot guess; the dash-row I2S floor in the winning cmplog seed's lineage confirms I2S participation.

When it spells 'Lab ' (0x4C616220) or another non-XYZ space, the switch on InPCS at line 429 falls through cmsSigXYZData and InPCS takes the false side of the case test, routing into the cmsSigLabData / default arms.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winning-resolving seeds carry a non-XYZ PCS literal at 0x10: seed 1/3/5 (cmplog) and seed 2 hold 'Lab' / 'MCH4Lab', and the BYTE DIFF shows W bytes 4c(L)/61(a)/62(b) at offsets 0x10/0x11/0x12 versus L bytes 52(R)/47(G)/42(B). The W per-role overlay reaches the cmsSigLabData arm (lines 454-477) that only executes on the false side of the XYZ case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 'RGB XYZ' at 0x10-0x17, so InPCS resolves to cmsSigXYZData and the true side of line 429 is taken (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L T=60 F=0 at line 429). The loser never reaches the Lab-input arm and the false branch stays globally blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted profile-signature CMP (the switch on InPCS compiles to integer comparisons against the cmsSigXYZData constant 0x58595A20), finds the matching 'XYZ ' bytes in the input, and substitutes the other observed operand ('Lab '/etc.) at offset 0x10, directly flipping the PCS field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reliably synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC at that offset; value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback gives no monotone path to a discrete FOURCC literal, so both losers stall.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked false side at line 429 is the XYZ-input case of a switch on InPCS, so the false side requires InPCS != cmsSigXYZData.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: case cmsSigXYZData: // Input profile operates in XYZ

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds resolve the branch by reaching the Lab-input arm (lines 454-477) which only runs on the false side of the XYZ case.
Winners carry a non-XYZ PCS literal 'Lab' at the profile-signature offset.
At offsets 0x10-0x12 winning bytes are L/a/b while losing bytes are R/G/B, the discriminating PCS field.
Loser seeds hold 'RGB XYZ' at the PCS field, making InPCS = cmsSigXYZData (true side).
Losers take only the true side of line 429 and never the false side.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by the I2S technique only.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes logged magic/tag-table CMP operands (profile header constants gating parametric curve Type 4 selection) into input bytes, assembling the multi-field profile structure; downstream mutation then drives the computed float e to a non-positive value, reaching the false side of e>0 that plain havoc cannot approach.

analysis.summary_one_line: An ICC profile embedding a Type-4 parametric tone curve whose coefficients drive e = Params[1]*R + Params[2] non-positive at R >= Params[4].

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes parsed into an ICC profile that selects parametric curve Type 4 (case 4, sRGB) and supplies Params[4]/Params[1]/Params[2] coefficients such that for some sample R>=Params[4] the linear form e = Params[1]*R + Params[2] evaluates to <= 0, taking the False side of the e>0 gate at cmsgamma.c:497. The structural precondition is a magic-byte-laden profile header (acsp 'acspAPPL', profile-class/colorspace tags) whose downstream tag table points at a Type-4 parametricCurveType.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning seeds carry the lcms/acsp profile skeleton with valid header magic and varying colorspace tags (RGB/Lab) at offsets 0x14-0x16, and they drive the blocker's enclosing function 941000 times vs 204000 for losers, with the False side actually taken (W F=59900 at line 497 vs L F=0); cmplog/vpc reached this via I2S substitution (dash-row ancestors).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds reach case 4 far less (DefaultEvalParametricFn 204000 invocations) and never take the False side (L 497 F=0): they hit the e>0 True side only, so the non-positive-e configuration of a Type-4 curve is never assembled by plain havoc/CMP_MAP mutation.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog I2SRandReplace substitutes logged CMP operands (the magic/tag-table integers gating the parametric-curve dispatch and the Type-4 selection) directly into the input bytes, assembling the multi-field profile structure that naive havoc and value_profile's Hamming-gradient feedback cannot synthesize, then letting downstream mutation drive e non-positive.

Loser-blocking seeds also carry the acsp profile skeleton but concentrate on XYZ colorspace and the same header magic.

The cmsGetPCS check compares the input's 0x14-0x17 word against the constant cmsSigLabData ('Lab '); cmplog logs that comparison and I2SRandReplace splices the logged 'Lab ' literal directly into the input bytes, flipping 'XYZ'->'Lab ' in one step. value_profile's CMP_MAP only buckets Hamming distance and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC, so it stays blocked.

This is the I2S roadblock-solving mechanism, matching claimed_mechanism I2SRandReplace.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature field of the ICC profile header (input offsets 0x10-0x16, rendered 'Lab ') must spell the Lab signature so T_COLORSPACE decodes to PT_Lab and the switch in _cmsICCcolorSpace selects case PT_Lab at cmspcs.c:781; loser inputs carrying 'RGB ' / 'XYZ ' fall through to non-Lab cases.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal bytes 4c 61 62 20 ('Lab ') at offsets 0x10-0x13 (and again at 0x14-0x16), so the parsed colorspace decodes to PT_Lab and the true side of the case PT_Lab branch is taken (W exclusively hits source line 781).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed instead holds 52 47 42 20 / 58 59 5a 20 ('RGB ' / 'XYZ ') at those same offsets, so the switch never reaches PT_Lab; L takes the default arm at line 805 and leaves line 781 unhit (T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the equality comparison of the parsed colorspace tag against the Lab constant, logs both operands, and splices the Lab signature bytes into the input at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so they must blindly mutate a 4-byte ASCII tag (effectively a magic-value match) by chance, which never happens in 10 trials. The mechanism is identical for both decisive pairs, hence single_feature.
